threats.title: Unvalidated User Input
threats.description: The system doesn't adequately validate user input, making it vulnerable to injection attacks (SQL injection, command injection, XSS). Malicious users can inject code or commands into input fields, potentially compromising the database, executing arbitrary commands on the server, or injecting malicious scripts into the application.
threats.attack_vector: Directly manipulating input fields in web forms, API requests, or other user-supplied data.
mitigations: Implement strict input validation and sanitization on all user-supplied data.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.

threats.title: Default Privileges and Unpatched Vulnerabilities
threats.description: The system is running with default configurations and likely contains unpatched vulnerabilities. Attackers can exploit these vulnerabilities to gain elevated privileges, allowing them to perform actions they shouldn't be authorized to do, such as installing malware, modifying system files, or gaining full control of the system.
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting known vulnerabilities in software, leveraging default credentials, misconfigured permissions.
mitigations: Regularly patch all software components with the latest security updates.
Conduct vulnerability scanning and penetration testing.
Implement a robust change management process to minimize the risk of misconfigurations.

threats.title: Weak Authentication Mechanism
threats.description: The system lacks documented authentication and authorization mechanisms, relying potentially on default configurations or weak passwords. This allows attackers to impersonate legitimate users or services, gaining unauthorized access to the system and its data.  The absence of multi-factor authentication (MFA) significantly amplifies this risk.
threats.attack_vector: Brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, password cracking, exploiting default credentials, social engineering.
mitigations: Implement strong password policies (length, complexity, rotation).
Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Regularly audit user accounts and permissions; disable inactive accounts.

threats.title: Exposed Sensitive Data
threats.description: Due to the lack of data classification and sensitivity information, it's unclear whether sensitive data (e.g., personal information, financial data) is being handled securely.  Unencrypted data at rest or in transit increases the risk of exposure through unauthorized access, theft, or interception.
threats.attack_vector: Unauthorized access to data stores, insecure network connections, vulnerabilities in data transmission protocols.
mitigations: Conduct a data classification exercise to identify and categorize sensitive data.
Implement data encryption at rest and in transit.
Enforce strict access controls based on the principle of least privilege.

threats.title: Lack of Audit Logging
threats.description: Insufficient audit logging makes it difficult to track user activity, identify security breaches, and determine accountability. Without detailed logs, it's nearly impossible to investigate incidents, demonstrate compliance, or identify malicious behavior.  The undocumented backup procedures further complicate incident response.
threats.attack_vector: Malicious actors can operate undetected, covering their tracks and denying responsibility for their actions.
mitigations: Implement comprehensive audit logging for all critical system events.
Regularly review audit logs for suspicious activity.
Establish a documented incident response plan and backup/recovery procedures.

gaps: Unvalidated User Input - Lack of comprehensive input validation controls
Default Privileges and Unpatched Vulnerabilities - Insufficient patching processes and configuration management
priorities: High - Unvalidated User Input & Weak Authentication Mechanism (Critical Severity)
Medium - Default Privileges and Unpatched Vulnerabilities (Critical Severity)

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,auth_service,core_api,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Unvalidated User Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Default Privileges and Unpatched Vulnerabilities
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Mechanism
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Exposed Sensitive Data
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Lack of Audit Logging
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-778
    owasp: A09:2021
    threat_category: Repudiation
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
